amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: E. coli survives extreme acid (pH 2-3) via three systems: AR1 (glucose-repressed, RpoS-dependent oxidative system), AR2 (glutamate-dependent, GadAB/GadC), AR3 (arginine-dependent, AdiA/AdiC). AR2 consumes cytoplasmic protons via GadA/B glutamate decarboxylases (Glu → GABA + CO2 + H+), exporting GABA via GadC antiporter. This maintains cytoplasmic pH ~4.2 in external pH 2. Regulators include RpoS, GadE/W/X, and EvgA/S two-component system.

name: Acid Resistance Systems (AR1, AR2, AR3)
notes: Shows very high OR gates (13) for multiple resistance systems. AR2 most effective, allows survival at pH 2 for hours.
scientificAccuracy: Based on comprehensive characterization of E. coli acid resistance mechanisms.

sources.title: Acid-sensing mechanism of glutamate-dependent acid resistance system in Escherichia coli
sources.authors: Gong S, Ma Z, Foster JW, Ni F, Zhou B.

sources.title: Regulation of acid resistance in Escherichia coli by the EvgAS two-component system
sources.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

sources.title: Escherichia coli acid resistance: systems and mechanisms
